description: Real-time Document Q&A API using Azure OpenAI and Azure Cosmos DB, exposed via Azure API Management with per-user rate limits. Defaults used where unspecified.

architecture.overview: Client apps call API Management endpoints which forward to a hosted API (Container App or Function) that performs retrieval from Cosmos DB and augmentation with Azure OpenAI. Per-user rate limits enforced in API Management using rate-limit-by-key or subscription quotas. Secrets stored in Key Vault. Application Insights for telemetry.
components: API host: Azure Container Apps (or Azure Functions) hosting the Q&A API
Azure API Management (frontend, routing, per-user rate limiting & authentication)

resources.name: qa-openai
resources.notes: Provision Azure OpenAI resource; use embedding + chat/completions models.

resources.name: qa-cosmosdb
resources.notes: Single-region Cosmos DB SQL API to store documents and embeddings. Defaults used for consistency (Session).

resources.name: qa-cosmosdb/qa-db
resources.notes: Database to contain containers for documents and metadata.

resources.name: qa-cosmosdb/qa-db/documents
resources.notes: Container holds original documents, metadata, and embedding vectors (if storing embeddings).

resources.name: qa-api-host
resources.notes: Host the Q&A API that queries Cosmos DB, calls Azure OpenAI for embeddings or completions, and returns answers in real-time. Defaults: 1 replica, autoscaling enabled.

resources.name: qa-apim
resources.notes: Expose public endpoints, apply policies for authentication and per-user rate limits (rate-limit-by-key using user id or subscription key).

resources.name: qa-keyvault
resources.notes: Store OpenAI keys, Cosmos DB connection strings, and other secrets.

resources.name: qa-appinsights
resources.notes: Telemetry for API host and APIM diagnostics.

security.auth: API Management will require JWT or subscription key. The API should validate tokens and use the user id claim for per-user policies.
security.secrets: Store sensitive keys in Key Vault and reference them from the API host and APIM.
security.network: Public endpoints for APIM; backend services restricted to inbound from APIM where possible.

scaling_and_cost.notes: Use default SKUs; enable autoscale on host. Monitor RU/s on Cosmos DB and scale as needed. Azure OpenAI cost depends on model usage.

deploymentSteps: Create resource group and .azure/infrastructure-plan.json (this plan) with status="draft".
Provision Cosmos DB, create DB & container.
Provision Azure OpenAI resource and validate model availability.
Deploy API host (Container App or Function). Configure to read secrets from Key Vault and connect to Cosmos DB.
Provision API Management, import API, configure policies for JWT validation and rate-limit-by-key per user.
Enable Application Insights for telemetry, wire up APIM diagnostics.
Run integration tests and performance tests.
notes: This plan is a draft and must be approved before generating IaC (bicep or terraform). Defaults used for region and SKUs per user instruction.
